description_long: This apartment building features a modern, angular design with a light-colored facade accented by blue-tinted windows and pastel panels. The structure is composed of multiple wings, creating a stepped and slightly curved layout, with a central vertical element that may house an elevator or stairwell. The building has four main floors, each with rows of regularly spaced windows and open balcony corridors, providing access to individual units. The ground level is partially open, suggesting possible parking or communal space beneath the residential floors. The roof is flat, with a raised central section that could contain mechanical equipment or a rooftop amenity. The overall appearance is clean and functional, typical of contemporary urban residential complexes.
description: A modern, multi-wing apartment building with blue-tinted windows, pastel panels, and open balcony corridors, featuring a flat roof and a stepped, angular layout.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main facade with open balcony corridors, blue-tinted windows, and a central vertical element.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the pastel-colored end walls and the stepped, angular shape of the building.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying more blue-tinted windows, a partially open ground level, and the central raised roof section.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with pastel panels and a clear view of the building's depth and angular layout.
attribute_window_arrangement: Windows are arranged in regular, evenly spaced vertical and horizontal rows, with some grouped in pairs or sets along the facade, creating a symmetrical and orderly appearance.
